items.summary: Darrell Cronk, Wells Fargo Wealth and Investment Management CIO, says zero interest rate hikes by the Federal Reserve are baked into their forecast that says the S&P 500 will reach 7,900 by the end of this year. He says markets are not in a selloff, he says this is a "rotation within equities." He speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: ‘War of words’ won’t stop Iran nuclear inspections, says IAEA
items.summary:
The international nuclear watchdog responsible for verifying Iran’s stockpile of near-bomb-grade uranium dismissed the conflicting signals from Tehran and Washington overnight and said it expects to resume full monitoring at some stage.
“There’s a war of words here,” said International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Director General Rafael Grossi, referring to White House statements about monitoring

Trump Orders DOJ to Look Into Gasoline Prices
items.summary: US President Donald Trump ordered the Department of Justice to look into gasoline prices, saying in a Truth Social post, “Prices better start going down a lot faster than what I’m seeing!” Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: RT by @MFA_China: Chinese FM Wang Yi met with Secretary-General of the UAE Supreme National Security Council Ali Mohammed Hamad Al Shamsi in New Delhi. #China stands ready to work with the #UAE to maintain high-level exchanges, fully unlock the potential of bilateral cooperation, promote people-to-people exchanges, and advance bilateral relations to a higher level. As the US–Israel–Iran conflict moves toward dialogue, whether genuine peace can be achieved still depends on joint efforts by all parties. At present, three priorities should be focused on: 1️⃣ Relevant parties should commit to a permanent and comprehensive ceasefire, and effectively implement the recently signed MoU. 2️⃣ Normal navigation through the Strait of Hormuz should be restored as soon as possible, keeping this “lifeline” for regional development open. 3️⃣ Countries in the region should draw lessons from repeated turmoil in the Middle East, rebuild mutual trust, and explore a new regional security architecture suited to the evolving situation. 🔗https://www.fmprc.gov.cn/wjbzhd/202606/t20260623_11950994.shtml
items.summary:
Chinese FM Wang Yi met with Secretary-General of the UAE Supreme National Security Council Ali Mohammed Hamad Al Shamsi in New Delhi.

#China stands ready to work with the #UAE to maintain high-level exchanges, fully unlock the potential of bilateral cooperation, promote people-to-people exchanges, and advance bilateral relations to a higher level.

As the US–Israel–Iran conflict moves toward dial

Met with UAE’s President @MohamedBinZayed in Abu Dhabi, where we discussed President Trump’s MOU with Iran, efforts to secure full and safe transit through the Strait of Hormuz, and regional stability. I thanked the UAE leadership for their unparalleled support, praised their courage and resilience in the face of Iran’s attacks, and reaffirmed our commitment to Emirati security and to our strong bilateral partnership.
